description_long: This object is a classic British-style red telephone booth, featuring a tall, rectangular structure with a flat roof and a prominent overhanging sign that reads 'TELEPHONE' in block letters above the door. The booth is painted a vivid red, with black window panes arranged in a grid pattern on the front-facing door, which is the only side with visible windows. The door has a metallic handle on the right side, and the rest of the booth's sides are solid red panels with no windows or openings. The top section above the door includes a small, gold-colored decorative element, adding a touch of detail to the otherwise utilitarian design. The booth is constructed primarily from metal, with glass or acrylic window panes and a likely hollow interior to accommodate a standing user and telephone equipment. This iconic design is commonly found on urban sidewalks and public spaces, serving as a functional and recognizable amenity.
description: A classic red British telephone booth with a grid-paned door, TELEPHONE signage, and solid red side panels. The booth features a metallic handle and a gold decorative accent above the door.
description_view_0: Front view showing the TELEPHONE sign, grid-paned door, and gold accent above the door.
description_view_1: Front-right angled view revealing the metallic door handle and the depth of the booth.
description_view_2: Front-left angled view similar to the front, but the door handle is less visible.
description_view_3: Rear view showing a solid red panel with no windows or signage.
attribute_window_configuration: one grid of rectangular windows on the door; no windows on other sides
attribute_signage_or_labeling: Gray TELEPHONE sign above the door with black block letters
